description: A streamed response is parsed or acted on before completion, so a truncated fragment is treated as a finished answer.
body: Failure mode: a streaming consumer parses structured output from accumulated chunks mid-stream or treats a mid-stream stop as the final answer, acting on incomplete data. Detection signals: structured parsing runs before the completion signal; the finish-reason is not checked for truncation; a stalled stream is consumed as a partial success. Do not parse partial JSON or treat truncation as a complete result. Safer fallback: accumulate chunks, render display text progressively but defer structured parsing until the stream signals completion, detect length-truncation explicitly, and treat truncation as a failure rather than a partial answer.
